evidence.scientific_clarity: Three methods plotted with mean ± 95% CI on log-scale y-axis, gap annotation at final step — fully satisfies brief.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=0 set; default DejaVu Sans used with no explicit font family override.
evidence.palette: Colors #888888/#4477AA/#EE6677 are Paul Tol colorblind-safe; linestyles (--/-./- ) additionally differentiate methods.
evidence.layout_density: 5.5×3.8 in figure with tight_layout; xlim extended 4 steps to fit annotation without crowding.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content; Δ=0.35 in caption matches computed annotation; legend placed upper-right where curves are well-separated.
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf is saved but script never sets rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42, so text is likely embedded as Type 3 (non-selectable/non-editable).
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial; figure.svg also produced as a bonus.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states n=5 seeds, 95% CI, log-scale, identifies OursModel as winner, and gives specific numerical finding Δ=0.35 at final step.

overall_strengths: Colorblind-safe Paul Tol palette combined with distinct linestyles ensures accessibility without relying on color alone.
Caption is publication-quality: quantifies the key finding (Δ=0.35), reports experimental details (n=5 seeds, 95% CI), and matches the figure annotation exactly.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype=0) means text in the PDF/SVG is not selectable or editable by journal typesetters.
Default DejaVu Sans font is used with no explicit font family set; most journals expect a serif or specific sans-serif (e.g., Helvetica/Arial) matching the manuscript.
